hazard.summary: Transient LLM provider failures (HTTP 500) can cause long-running tasks to hang or drop without retry, resulting in silent reliability loss.
signals: Task hang without retry, logs output 'LLM call failed'
Retryable errors suggested: 500/502/503/429/network timeout; do not retry 401/403/400 (except 429)

constraints: LLM/tool calls must classify errors into retryable vs terminal (e.g., retry 5xx/429/timeouts; do not retry 4xx except 429).
Retry behavior must be bounded by a retry budget (max attempts and/or max elapsed time) to avoid unbounded churn.
Backoff must use exponential delay with jitter to avoid synchronized retry storms.
When Retry-After is provided (429), backoff must honor it.
If retries are exhausted, the system must surface a user-facing failure state (no silent task drop/hang).
verification: Inject transient failures (HTTP 500 / network timeout) during a long task and confirm the agent retries with exponential backoff + jitter instead of dropping.
Inject a terminal auth error (401/403) and confirm the agent fails fast without retry.
Inject 429 with Retry-After and confirm the agent delays according to Retry-After.
Confirm logs include retry attempt count and final outcome (success or exhausted).
Confirm the user sees a clear message when retries are exhausted (no silent hang/drop).
notes: This receipt captures a common agent reliability failure mode: treating LLM calls as 'single shot' despite expected transient failures (5xx/429/timeouts). The mitigation is a bounded, configurable retry policy with error classification and user-visible status when retries are occurring or exhausted.
